question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
Here is a complete `charmcraft.yaml` for a Kubernetes charm, with every required field and the most important optional ones annotated:

```yaml
# charmcraft.yaml — Kubernetes Charm Example

# ── Identity ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

type: charm                          # Always "charm" (required)

name: my-app-k8s                     # Charmhub slug; -k8s suffix is convention
                                     # for Kubernetes charms (required)

title: My Application (K8s)          # Human-readable name on Charmhub (recommended)

summary: Deploys My Application on Kubernetes.   # Max 78 chars (required)

description: |                       # Shown on Charmhub; supports multiline (required)
  This charm deploys and manages My Application as a sidecar
  container on a Kubernetes cluster using the Juju sidecar pattern.
  It supports configuration of the HTTP port, log level, and more.

# ── Platform / Build ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

base: ubuntu@24.04                   # OS base for the charm code itself (required)

parts:
  my-app-k8s:
    plugin: charm                    # Use the built-in charm plugin
    charm-entrypoint: src/charm.py   # Default, shown explicitly for clarity
    charm-requirements:
      - requirements.txt             # Python deps installed into the charm

# ── Kubernetes-Specific: Assumes ──────────────────────────────────────────────

assumes:                             # Declare minimum Juju/feature requirements
  - k8s-api                         # This charm REQUIRES a Kubernetes cloud
  - juju >= 3.5                     # Minimum Juju version

# ── Kubernetes-Specific: Containers & OCI Images ─────────────────────────────

containers:                          # REQUIRED for K8s sidecar charms
  my-app:                            # Container name (referenced in charm code)
    resource: my-app-image           # Points to an entry in `resources` below
    mounts:
      - storage: data-volume         # References a `storage` entry below
        location: /var/lib/my-app    # Mount path inside the container
    uid: 1000                        # Rootless operation (Juju 3.5+)
    gid: 1000

resources:                           # REQUIRED — one oci-image per container
  my-app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for My Application
    # upstream-source is optional but useful for CI/local testing:
    upstream-source: ghcr.io/my-org/my-app:latest

# ── Privilege Level ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

charm-user: non-root                 # Recommended; "root", "sudoer", or "non-root"
                                     # Requires uid/gid set on containers above

# ── User Configuration ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

config:
  options:
    http-port:
      type: int
      default: 8080
      description: Port the application listens on.
    log-level:
      type: string
      default: info
      description: "Log verbosity: debug, info, warn, error."
    feature-flag:
      type: boolean
      default: false
      description: Enable experimental features.

# ── Relations ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

provides:
  http:
    interface: http                  # What this charm offers to others
    optional: false

requires:
  database:
    interface: pgsql                 # postgresql relation interface
    limit: 1                        # Only one DB allowed
    optional: true
  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    limit: 1
    optional: true

peers:
  replicas:
    interface: my-app-peers          # Used for peer-to-peer charm coordination

# ── Storage ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

storage:
  data-volume:                       # Referenced in containers.mounts above
    type: filesystem
    description: Persistent storage for application data.
    location: /var/lib/my-app        # Default mount path
    minimum-size: 1G
    multiple: 1-5                    # Allow 1 to 5 storage instances

# ── Actions ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

actions:
  rotate-secret:
    description: Rotate the application secret key.
    additionalProperties: false      # Always set explicitly
  reset-database:
    description: Wipe and reinitialise the application database.
    params:
      confirm:
        type: boolean
        description: Must be true to proceed.
    required:
      - confirm
    additionalProperties: false

# ── Charm Libraries ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

charm-libs:
  - lib: traefik_k8s.ingress         # charm_name.library_name
    version: "2"
  - lib: data_platform_libs.data_interfaces
    version: "0.39"

# ── Metadata / Links ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

links:
  documentation: https://discourse.charmhub.io/t/my-app-k8s
  issues: https://github.com/my-org/my-app-k8s-operator/issues
  source: https://github.com/my-org/my-app-k8s-operator
  contact: My Org <charms@my-org.example.com>
```

---

## Key Rules to Remember

| Rule | Detail |
|---|---|
| **`containers` is K8s-only and required** | Every container needs a matching `oci-image` entry in `resources` |
| **`assumes: k8s-api`** | Prevents accidental deployment on a machine (VM/bare-metal) cloud |
| **`-k8s` name suffix** | Strong convention on Charmhub to distinguish K8s from machine charms |
| **`charm-user: non-root`** | Best practice; pair with `uid`/`gid` on the container for rootless pods |
| **No `actions.yaml` / `metadata.yaml`** | Charmcraft generates these from `charmcraft.yaml` — don't create them manually |
| **`parts.plugin: charm`** | Tells Charmcraft how to build the Python charm; `charm-requirements` wires in your `requirements.txt` |
